comment: Beginner open chords. strings order = low E (6th) to high E (1st). fret: -1 muted, 0 open, >0 fretted. finger: 0 none, 1 index .. 4 pinky. Diagrams mirror for left-handed mode (rule e).

chords.name: Em
chords.tip: A perfect first chord — just two fingers, and strum all six strings.

chords.name: Am
chords.tip: Don't strum the thickest (low E) string. Keep your fingers curled.

chords.name: D
chords.tip: Strum from the D string down — the two thickest strings stay quiet.

chords.name: E
chords.tip: Same shape as E minor, with one extra finger. Strum all six strings.

chords.name: A
chords.tip: Three fingers in a little row on the second fret. Skip the low E.

chords.name: Dm
chords.tip: A softer, sadder D. Strum from the D string down.

chords.name: C
chords.tip: Stretch your ring finger to the third fret. Skip the low E string.

chords.name: G
chords.tip: Spread your fingers across the neck and strum all six strings.

chords.name: G7
chords.tip: A bluesy G. Often used to lead nicely into a C chord.
